items.title: Model Complexity
items.description: max_depth, min_samples_leaf, and min_samples_split are direct complexity knobs on CART. Shallow trees underfit (high bias); deep trees overfit small tabular sets (high variance). Model Complexity owns the bias–variance framing — this topic owns the tree-specific hyperparameters on that curve.

items.title: Random Forests
items.description: Random forests train many decorrelated CART trees on bootstrap samples with random feature subsets, then aggregate votes or averages. Mastery of single-tree splits, impurity, and depth limits is prerequisite — forests inherit CART mechanics but reduce variance through ensembling.

items.title: Regression vs Classification
items.description: The same CART framework builds classifiers (Gini/entropy, majority vote leaves) and regressors (variance/MSE, mean leaves). Problem type determines impurity measure, leaf value, and evaluation metrics — not the recursive split structure itself.

items.title: Encoding
items.description: Standard CART implementations split on numeric thresholds. Categorical features must be one-hot encoded, label-encoded with care, or target-encoded before training — otherwise the tree cannot consume raw string columns. Encoding owns representation; this topic consumes encoded numeric matrices.

items.title: Entropy (information theory)
items.description: Tree entropy impurity H = −Σ p log p shares the Shannon entropy formula. Information-theory Entropy owns the general definition; this topic applies it as a node impurity and split criterion in CART.
